claim: FBI IC3 2024 reported a record $16.6 billion in losses across 859,532 complaints, a 33% jump over 2023.
quote: complaints totaled 859,532, with losses of $16.6 billion, representing a 33 percent increase from 2023

claim: Cyber-enabled fraud was 38% of IC3 2024 complaints but 83% of losses ($13.7B).
quote: cyber-enabled fraud accounted for 38 percent of all complaints received in 2024 and was responsible for a staggering 83 percent of the total financial losses reported

claim: BEC losses in 2024 were $2.77B across 21,442 incidents; investment fraud was the costliest category.
quote: In 2024 alone, BEC losses totaled $2.77 billion across 21,442 reported incidents

claim: Investment fraud topped IC3 2024 categories at roughly $6.57B; crypto-related complaints reached 149,686 with $9.3B losses (+66% YoY).
quote: in 2024, IC3 logged 149,686 cryptocurrency-related complaints, with total reported costs exceeding $9.3 billion—a 66% increase over 2023

claim: Elder fraud (60+) reported $4.9B in losses in 2024, up 43% YoY.
quote: Elder fraud across all scam types rose to $4.9 billion in reported losses in 2024—a 43% increase year-over-year

claim: FTC consumers reported $12.5B lost to fraud in 2024, up 25% YoY; investment scams led at $5.7B (+24%); imposter scams second at $2.95B.
quote: Consumers reported losing more than $12.5 billion to fraud last year, representing a 25% increase over the previous year. Investment scams accounted for the most fraud losses at $5.7 billion

claim: Government-imposter scam losses alone climbed $171M in 2024 to $789M, the leading FTC reported scam category.
quote: losses to government imposter scams in particular increased $171 million from 2023 to a total of $789 million in 2024

claim: Deloitte Center for Financial Services projects gen-AI-enabled US fraud losses growing from $12.3B (2023) to $40B by 2027 — 32% CAGR.
quote: gen AI could enable fraud losses to reach US$40 billion in the United States by 2027, from US$12.3 billion in 2023, a compound annual growth rate of 32%

claim: Sumsub 2025-2026 report: sophisticated multi-step fraud rose 180% YoY; multi-step share grew from 10% (2024) to 28% (2025).
quote: +180% YoY growth in 'sophisticated' fraud: the share of multi-step attacks rose from 10% (2024) to 28% (2025)

claim: Sumsub measured global identity fraud at 2.2% of verifications in 2025 (vs 2.6% in 2024); Maldives saw a 2,100% YoY jump in deepfake attacks.
quote: identity fraud attempts at 2.2% of all analyzed verifications worldwide – compared to 2.6% in 2024 ... suffered a 2100% year-on-year increase in deepfake attacks

claim: Chainalysis: 2025 crypto scam losses hit $17B; AI-enabled scams generated 4.5x more revenue per operation; impersonation scams grew 1,400% YoY; average scam payment rose 253% to $2,764.
quote: crypto scams generated over $17 billion in losses for 2025 ... Impersonation scams grew more than 1,400% year over year, driven in part by AI tools ... AI-enabled scams generated 4.5 times more revenue per operation than traditional scams

claim: Chainalysis 2024 data: pig-butchering / romance-baiting revenue grew ~40% YoY and deposits to those scams jumped ~210%.
quote: the number of deposits to pig butchering scammers increased 210% YoY in 2024

claim: UK Finance H1 2025: APP fraud losses £257.5M (+12% YoY); investment-scam losses £97.7M (+55%); romance scam losses +35%.
quote: APP fraud losses were £257.5 million in the first half of 2025, representing a 12% increase ... Investment scams led the surge, accounting for £97.7 million in losses — a 55% increase

claim: TRM Labs (Congressional testimony): FinCEN-designated Cambodian Huione Group received $39.6B in transaction volume; Chinese-language laundering networks processed $103B in 2025 (up from $123M in 2020).
quote: FinCEN's designation of Huione Group (a Cambodia-based network) as a primary money laundering concern in 2025 identified that organization as receiving USD 39.6 billion in transaction volume

claim: ElevenLabs Instant Voice Cloning recommends ~1-2 minutes of clean audio; 30-second samples can yield excellent results, while >3 minutes can be detrimental. Professional Voice Cloning wants ~1-3 hours.
quote: Approximately 1-2 minutes of clear audio without any reverb, artifacts, or background noise of any kind is recommended... users have seen samples of only 30 seconds and get excellent results... avoid recording more than 3 minutes.

claim: Modern AI voice tools can generate a convincing voice model from as little as 3 seconds of audio scraped from TikTok, voicemail greetings, YouTube or Instagram, with ~90% accuracy.
quote: low-cost AI tools can clone a human voice with nearly 90% accuracy using a sample of just a few seconds of audio — often scraped from a Facebook video, a TikTok post, or a voicemail

claim: In April 2024 a LastPass employee received WhatsApp calls, texts and a voicemail using a deepfake of CEO Karim Toubba's voice; the employee ignored it and reported it, with zero impact.
quote: one of our employees received a series of calls, texts, and at least one voicemail featuring an audio deepfake from a threat actor impersonating our CEO via WhatsApp

claim: The LastPass deepfake attempt used WhatsApp — outside normal LastPass business channels — which tipped off the employee.
quote: the use of WhatsApp as a communication channel... is also outside of normal business communication channels

claim: The FCC finalized a $6M fine in September 2024 against Steve Kramer for the AI-generated Biden robocall sent to NH primary voters in January 2024; Lingo Telecom paid $1M for distributing it.
quote: FCC Issues $6M Fine For N.H. Robocalls

claim: Kramer was indicted on 13 felony counts of voter suppression and 13 misdemeanor counts of impersonating a candidate in NH; he claimed the deepfake was a 'warning' about AI dangers.
quote: 13 counts of voter suppression, a felony, and 13 counts of impersonating a candidate, a misdemeanor

claim: OpenAI Voice Engine — first previewed in early 2024 with a 15-second sample claim — remained unreleased as of 2025-2026, restricted to 'trusted partners' over abuse fears.
quote: the tool remains in preview roughly a year after its initial announcement, with OpenAI giving no indication as to when it might launch

claim: OpenAI's Voice Engine ships with watermarking, requires speaker consent, and demands clear AI-disclosure to listeners, citing election-year risks.
quote: Safety measures like watermarking and proactive monitoring are in place... developers must obtain 'explicit consent' from the original speaker

claim: ElevenLabs operates a 'No-Go Voices' classifier that blocks creation of clones approximating presidential candidates and other high-risk public figures.
quote: restricts the creation of voice clones that approximate the voices of political figures, including those actively involved in presidential elections in the US and UK

claim: Pindrop Pulse detects synthetic and bot speech in real time from ~2 seconds of audio, claiming up to 99.2% accuracy and <1% false-positive rate when paired with multifactor authentication.
quote: Pindrop detects synthetic and bot-generated speech in real time using just two seconds of audio, with up to 99.2% accuracy

claim: In an NPR-run benchmark, Pindrop Pulse correctly identified 81 of 84 deepfake audio samples (96.4% accuracy), beating other commercial detectors.
quote: Pindrop® Pulse emerged as a clear leader, boasting a 96.4% accuracy rate in identifying AI-generated audio, detecting 81 out of 84 audio samples correctly

claim: McAfee's 7,000-person global survey found 70% of people were not confident they could distinguish a cloned voice from the real one; 1 in 4 had experienced a voice-clone scam or knew someone who had, and 77% of targeted victims lost money.
quote: 70% of people in a worldwide survey said they weren't confident they could tell the difference between a cloned voice and the real thing

claim: A finance worker at engineering firm Arup in Hong Kong was tricked into wiring $25M across 15 transactions in January 2024 after a video conference where deepfakes posed as the UK-based CFO and other colleagues.
quote: A finance worker at a multinational firm was tricked into paying out $25 million to fraudsters using deepfake technology to pose as the company's chief financial officer in a video conference call

claim: In March 2025, a finance director at a Singapore multinational authorized a US$499,000 payment after a Zoom meeting in which the CFO and other executives were entirely AI-generated.
quote: a finance director at a multinational firm in Singapore authorised a US$499,000 payment during what appeared to be a Zoom call with the company's senior leadership. None of the people on the call were real.

claim: Deepfake-enabled vishing surged ~1,600% in Q1 2025 vs late 2024, and dark-web markets sell turnkey vishing-as-a-service from a $1,000 base price with voice-cloning kits and LLM wrappers tuned to bypass filters.
quote: Deepfake-enabled vishing surged by over 1,600% in the first quarter of 2025

claim: The FBI reported $893M in 2025 losses tied to AI-related scams (voice clones for 'family in distress' calls and investment deepfakes), with older adults accounting for $352M; AARP estimates 1-in-5 over-65s targeted, average victim age 74, average loss $18,000.
quote: $893 million in losses tied to AI-related scams, including voice cloning for 'family in distress' calls and deepfakes in investment schemes, with older adults accounting for $352 million

claim: NCC Group researchers built a real-time voice-cloning vishing rig in about an hour using only publicly available short audio clips and off-the-shelf hardware, then successfully extracted sensitive info from real organizations in red-team engagements.
quote: trained an AI voice cloning tool using a publicly available, short audio clip and within about an hour managed to develop a vishing system that could mimic any voice in real-time

claim: Mandiant's M-Trends 2026 reports voice phishing climbed to the second-most-common initial infection vector in 2025, present in 11% of investigations where a vector could be identified.
quote: voice phishing climbed to the second-most common initial infection vector in 2025, appearing in 11% of Mandiant investigations

claim: The victim of the HK$200M deepfake video-call fraud was British engineering and design firm Arup; Arup confirmed fake voices and images were used and notified Hong Kong police in January 2024.
quote: Arup, a British multinational design and engineering company known for designing the Sydney Opera House... told CNN on Friday that it notified Hong Kong police in January about the fraud incident, and confirmed that fake voices and images were used.

claim: The Arup deepfake attack used existing online conference footage to synthesize the CFO and colleagues; the lone real participant was the targeted finance worker.
quote: Hong Kong police determined that the perpetrators developed AI generated deepfakes of the finance worker's CFO and colleagues by leveraging existing video and audio files of these individuals from online conferences and virtual company meetings.

claim: WPP CEO Mark Read was the target of a May 2024 deepfake Microsoft Teams attack using a WhatsApp account with a public photo, AI-cloned voice, and YouTube footage; the scam failed.
quote: Hackers attempted to defraud WPP executives by using deepfake and AI voice cloning technology to impersonate CEO Mark Read.

claim: The WPP attack used a fake WhatsApp account, a Teams meeting, AI voice cloning of Mark Read, and chat-function impersonation to solicit money and personal details from another executive.
quote: The scammers then arranged a Microsoft Teams meeting, impersonating Read both visually and vocally... fraudsters utilised the chat function to further impersonate Read, trying to solicit money and personal details from the targeted executive.

claim: On 23 July 2024 KnowBe4 disclosed it had hired a North Korean fake IT worker who used a stolen US identity, an AI-enhanced photo, and passed four video interviews; malware loaded on the issued laptop within minutes of receipt.
quote: We sent them their Mac workstation, and the moment it was received, it immediately started to load malware... HR conducted four video conference based interviews on separate occasions, confirming the individual matched the photo provided on their application.

claim: Mandiant and FBI investigation confirmed the KnowBe4 North Korean operative used deepfake technology and a VPN; KnowBe4 was not breached because the new-hire account had limited permissions.
quote: An investigation conducted with Mandiant and the FBI showed that the threat actor used deepfake technology to obtain the job and a VPN to manipulate their location.

claim: In December 2024 the DOJ indicted 14 DPRK nationals for an IT-worker scheme that generated over $88 million for North Korea's weapons programs over six years.
quote: North Korea's fake IT worker scam hauled in $88M

claim: Microsoft tracks the DPRK IT-worker cluster as Jasper Sleet (overlaps with 'Famous Chollima'); operators use AI-generated synthetic identities, deepfakes for interviews, and laptop farms inside the US.
quote: Jasper Sleet: North Korean remote IT workers' evolving tactics to infiltrate organizations

claim: FBI PSA July 2025 warns DPRK IT workers use real-time deepfake video and stolen identities to gain remote employment at US firms; by November 2025 DOJ reported 136 victim companies and seized $15M in related crypto.
quote: North Korean IT Worker Threats to U.S. Businesses

claim: DeepFaceLive — a Windows real-time face-swap tool for streaming and video calls — has 30.8k GitHub stars and was archived by its owner on 13 Nov 2024.
quote: Real-time face swap for PC streaming or video calls. This repository was archived by the owner on Nov 13, 2024. It is now read-only.

claim: Deep-Live-Cam — successor-style 'real-time face swap and one-click video deepfake with only a single image' — has 92.4k GitHub stars and is actively maintained, dominating the live face-swap category.
quote: real time face swap and one-click video deepfake with only a single image

claim: The original Faceswap project ('Deepfakes Software For All') sits at ~55.1k GitHub stars and 13.4k forks, the foundational training-based deepfake toolkit.
quote: Deepfakes Software For All

claim: Roop, the popular one-click face-swap repo, reached ~30.5k stars before being archived in March 2026 with the author citing concerns about 'second-order effects' of the software.
quote: When I originally built this project, I approached it primarily as a technical experiment in generative media. Not long after releasing it, my view of the broader second-order effects around software in this category changed.

claim: Resemble AI's Q1 2025 report tallies more than $200 million in deepfake-enabled fraud losses in the first four months of 2025, with video the dominant modality (46%) followed by images (32%) and audio (22%).
quote: Deepfake-Enabled Fraud Has Already Caused $200 Million in Financial Losses in 2025

claim: Sumsub's 2025–26 Identity Fraud Report finds deepfake fraud up roughly 700% globally Q1 2024–Q1 2025, deepfakes now ~7% of all fraud attempts, and 76% of fraud attempts occur after KYC verification.
quote: A staggering 700% global increase in deepfake fraud occurred between Q1 2024 and Q1 2025... Deepfakes now represent 7% of global fraudulent activity.

claim: Reality Defender's 2025 video-detection model upgrade claims an 8% improvement in balanced accuracy over the prior model with broader generator coverage; Gartner named it a 2025 market leader in deepfake detection.
quote: Reality Defender's new video model delivers an 8% improvement in balanced accuracy, along with support for a greater number of closed- and open-source deepfake generation platforms.

claim: Microsoft's Video Authenticator (released 2020) returns a real-time confidence score by detecting deepfake blending boundaries; Microsoft has not published a current accuracy figure for the tool.
quote: Video Authenticator can analyze a still photo or video to provide a percentage chance, or confidence score, that the media is artificially manipulated.

claim: IBM X-Force 2023 study: ChatGPT generated a phishing email in 5 minutes vs ~16 hours for human red team; AI email got 11% click rate vs 14% for human-written, sent to 800+ healthcare employees.
quote: X-Force researchers used ChatGPT to craft a 'highly convincing' phishing email in just five minutes... The phishing email written by humans achieved a click rate of 14%, while the AI-generated email achieved an 11% click rate.

claim: IBM X-Force found human-crafted phishing beat AI-generated phishing by only 3 percentage points (14% vs 11% click rate), and 59% of recipients reported the AI-generated email as suspicious vs 52% for human-written.
quote: Phishing emails written by humans have a 3% better click rate than phishing emails written by ChatGPT... The research project was performed at one global healthcare company based in Canada.

claim: Hoxhunt's March 2025 study found AI spear-phishing agent 'JKR' was 24% more effective than elite human red teams across 70,000 simulations against 2.5M+ users — a 55% relative improvement vs 2023 when humans led by 31%.
quote: From 2023 to 2025, AI's phishing performance relative to elite human red teams improved by 55%... March 2025: Human failure rate: 2.25%, AI failure rate: 2.78%, Result: AI performed 23.3% more effectively.

claim: Hoxhunt 2026 Phishing Trends Report: AI-generated phishing rose from 4% of reported emails in November 2025 to 56% by December 2025 — a 14x surge — alongside a 50x rise in malicious SVG attachments and 500% rise in callback phishing in Q4 2025.
quote: AI-generated phishing campaigns have flooded inboxes with a 14x surge since December, and now represent around half of all attacks reported by users.

claim: Microsoft Digital Defense Report 2025 (released Oct 30, 2025): AI-generated phishing emails achieve a 54% click rate vs 12% for manually written — 4.5x more effective — and AI-enhanced campaigns can be up to 50x more profitable.
quote: AI-generated phishing emails achieve a 54% click rate versus 12% for manually written emails—a 4.5x higher effectiveness rate... AI-enhanced attacks can be up to 50 times more profitable.

claim: Microsoft 2025 MDDR: ClickFix social engineering became the most common initial access vector, accounting for 47% of Defender Expert notifications in late 2024-2025; 28% of breaches start with phishing or social engineering.
quote: ClickFix became the most common initial access technique in Defender Expert notifications in late 2024-2025, accounting for 47% of cases.

claim: WormGPT 4 launched on Telegram and DarknetArmy ~Sept 27, 2025 with $50/month or $220 lifetime pricing (source code included); generates functional ransomware PowerShell scripts on demand and has 500+ subscribers in its sales channel.
quote: Subscriptions for WormGPT 4 start at $50 for monthly access and rise to $220 for lifetime access, which includes full source code.

claim: Cato Networks discovered new 2024-2025 WormGPT variants are jailbroken wrappers around commercial LLMs (xAI Grok, Mistral Mixtral) sold on BreachForums for ~€60, posted Feb 25, 2025 by 'keanu'.
quote: New WormGPT variants have emerged, built on top of commercial LLMs like xAI's Grok and Mistral's Mixtral, with subscription models starting around €60.

claim: Palo Alto Unit 42 documents the malicious-LLM ecosystem expanding from WormGPT/FraudGPT (2023) to DarkBERT, DarkGPT, PoisonGPT, WolfGPT, and KawaiiGPT (a free GitHub-distributed entry-level malicious LLM identified July 2025).
quote: KawaiiGPT, first identified in July 2025 and currently at version 2.5, represents an accessible, entry-level malicious LLM. It is freely available on GitHub.

claim: Anthropic disclosed (Nov 13, 2025) the first documented large-scale AI-orchestrated cyberattack: a Chinese state-sponsored group used Claude Code to autonomously target ~30 global organizations, with AI performing 80-90% of the campaign and humans intervening at only 4-6 decision points.
quote: The threat actor was able to use AI to perform 80-90% of the campaign, with human intervention required only sporadically (perhaps 4-6 critical decision points per hacking campaign).

claim: OpenAI's Feb 2024 threat report banned accounts tied to North Korean Emerald Sleet, who used ChatGPT to draft spear-phishing content in English/Chinese/Japanese targeting South Korean diplomatic missions and APAC defense experts.
quote: Emerald Sleet used OpenAI's services to identify experts and organizations focused on defense issues in the Asia-Pacific region... and draft content that could be used in phishing campaigns.

claim: Symantec demonstrated in Feb 2025 that OpenAI's Operator agent (launched Jan 23, 2025) could be prompted to identify a target, find their email, write a PowerShell payload, and send a convincing phishing lure end-to-end with minimal social-engineering of the agent itself.
quote: Symantec researchers used OpenAI's Operator agent... and asked the agent to identify a person, find their email address, create a PowerShell script, and 'email it to them using a convincing lure.'

claim: Verizon 2025 DBIR: pretexting (the core BEC technique) accounts for 30% of social engineering incidents, nearly doubled year-over-year and overtaking phishing in some categories; FBI median BEC loss is ~$50,000 per complaint.
quote: Phishing was used in 57% of social engineering incidents, followed by pretexting in 30%... Pretexting incidents the core of BEC have nearly doubled.

claim: UNODC's April 2025 Inflection Point report estimates Southeast-Asian scam centres generate just under US$40 billion in annual profits and that close to 7,000 workers from over 50 countries were freed in a Myanmar crackdown.
quote: hundreds of industrial-scale scam centres generating just under US $40 billion in annual profits, according to latest UNODC estimates ... close to 7,000 workers from more than 50 countries were freed from crime syndicates

claim: UN reporting documents torture, rape and forced labor inside multi-billion-dollar Southeast-Asian scam centres staffed by trafficked workers from more than 50 countries.
quote: UN report exposes torture, rape in Southeast Asia's multi-billion-dollar scam centres

claim: INTERPOL's largest-ever 2024 anti-trafficking-fuelled-fraud operation across 116 countries arrested over 2,500 people linked to scam compounds running romance, investment and crypto fraud.
quote: INTERPOL's largest-ever global operation targeting trafficking-fuelled fraud across 116 countries and territories led to the arrest of over 2,500 individuals ... voice phishing, romance scams, investment fraud and cryptocurrency scams

claim: INTERPOL launched Operation Shadow Storm in 2026, a UK-Home-Office-funded task force to dismantle scam centres, citing crypto flows linked to compounds exceeding $11 billion.
quote: INTERPOL launches Operation Shadow Storm to fight scam centres ... Crypto flows linked to scam compounds exceed $11 billion

claim: Chainalysis found pig-butchering crypto revenue grew ~40% YoY in 2024 with deposits up ~210%, while AI service vendors on the Huione marketplace showed a 1,900% compound annual growth rate from 2021-2024.
quote: pig butchering revenue grew nearly 40% year over year, with the number of deposits to pig butchering scams growing nearly 210% ... AI service vendors seeing a staggering 1,900% compound annual growth rate from 2021-2024

claim: Chainalysis estimated 2024 crypto scams at $9.9 billion likely revising up to $12.4 billion as more scam wallets get identified.
quote: cryptocurrency scams amounted to $9.9 billion in 2024, with that figure likely to be revised higher. It predicts 2024's figure to grow to a record of $12.4 billion

claim: Chainalysis 2025 figures show crypto scam revenue reached at least $14 billion on-chain, potentially exceeding $17 billion as more wallets are identified, with AI tools amplifying losses.
quote: Crypto scams and fraud took in at least $14 billion on-chain in 2025, up from a revised total of $12 billion the previous year, and the 2025 figure could exceed $17 billion

claim: Chinese actor Wang Xing was lured to Mae Sot on a fake film audition on 3 January 2025, kidnapped into Myawaddy, and rescued on 7 January after Thai authorities responded to his family's report.
quote: Wang Xing went missing on January 3, 2025, in Mae Sot, lured by cyber scam operators under the false pretense of a film audition, and was kidnapped and transported to a scam compound across the Myanmar border in Myawaddy

claim: After the Wang Xing case, Thailand cut electricity, internet and fuel to Myawaddy scam hubs on 5 February 2025, and Myanmar repatriated 261 victims and detained 1,303 foreign nationals between 12-17 February 2025.
quote: February 5: Thailand cut electricity, internet, and fuel to scam hubs in Myawaddy. February 12-17: Myanmar repatriated 261 trafficked victims, and authorities detained 1,303 foreign nationals

claim: In November 2025, OFAC sanctioned Burma's DKBA militia and the Trans Asia/Troth Star/Chamu Sawang network linked to the KK Park and Huanya scam compounds in Myawaddy, citing $10 billion in 2024 American losses to SE Asia scams (66% YoY).
quote: Trans Asia has entered into land lease contracts with other entities—including the OFAC-designated KNA and Troth Star—to develop other scam compounds, including the Huanya compound and the notorious KK Park compound ... Americans lost at least $10 billion in 2024 ... a 66 percent increase

claim: In May 2025, OFAC designated the Karen National Army militia, leader Saw Chit Thu, and his sons as a transnational criminal organization for facilitating cyber scams, human trafficking and smuggling in Burma.
quote: OFAC sanctioned the Karen National Army (KNA), a militia group in Burma, as a transnational criminal organization, along with the group's leader Saw Chit Thu, and his two sons

claim: NCMEC's 2025 CyberTipline received 21.3 million reports and 1.4 million online-enticement reports (a 156% jump from 2024), plus over 1.5 million reports linked to generative AI and child exploitation.
quote: the CyberTipline received 21.3 million reports ... 1.4 million reports of online enticement in 2025, a 156% increase from 2024 ... more than 1.5 million CyberTipline reports indicating a nexus to GAI

claim: NCMEC has tracked at least 36 teenage boys who died by suicide since 2021 after sextortion victimization, including a 17-year-old whose extortion lasted 19.5 hours and 400 messages.
quote: since 2021, NCMEC is aware of at least 36 teenage boys who have taken their lives because they were victimized by sextortion ... the online exploitation lasted 19½ hours and involved 400 messages

claim: Two Nigerian brothers, Samuel and Sampson Ogoshi, were sentenced to 17.5 years for the sextortion of Marquette teen Jordan DeMay, who killed himself within hours after being demanded $1,000 (he could only send $300).
quote: Two Nigerian men were sentenced 210 months each (17.5 years) ... The person on the other end demanded DeMay send $1,000 ... But DeMay could only come up with $300. Hours later, he died by suicide

claim: In 2025 deepfake fraud in the US jumped 700% in Q1 and global deepfake-related losses topped $200 million in Q1 alone, with scammers running real-time face-swap and AI voice on Zoom/WhatsApp video calls.
quote: Deepfake fraud in the U.S. skyrocketed by 700% in the first few months of 2025. Deepfake fraud globally caused more than $200 million in financial losses in just the first quarter of 2025

claim: Haotian, a face-swapping AI app behind 2025 romance scams, lets users tune up to 50 facial settings to mimic a target's appearance and was monetised largely through crypto payments.
quote: Haotian, a face-swapping AI app, has been implicated in romance scams ... Users can customize an extensive range of facial features—up to 50 different settings

claim: The 2024 US TIP Report kept Cambodia and Burma on Tier 3, citing tens of thousands of forced-labor victims in scam compounds, senior officials profiting from the operations, and ~220,000 trafficked into the syndicates per a 2023 UN figure.
quote: exploitation of tens of thousands of victims in forced criminality in online scam operations in Cambodia ... Some senior government officials and advisors owned properties and facilities known to be utilized by online scam operators

claim: The FBI has warned that AI deepfakes and 'nudify' apps are powering a 322% spike in sextortion cases (Feb 2022-Feb 2023), with male victims aged 14-17 most targeted and an alarming linked suicide count.
quote: horrific increase in sextortion cases targeting US minors, with victims typically males between the ages of 14 and 17 ... sextortion cases, which have increased 322%

claim: In October 2025 US prosecutors seized $15 billion in crypto from pig-butchering operations tied to Cambodian forced-labor camps run by Chen Zhi's network, dwarfing the prior $225 million record seizure.
quote: Feds seize $15 billion in crypto from 'pig butchering' ... Chen and others allegedly operated at least 10 forced labor camps across Cambodia

claim: C2PA / Content Credentials had over 6,000 members and affiliates by January 2026, with Adobe writing credentials across all Creative Cloud products and Firefly automatically.
quote: over 6,000 members and affiliates have joined the C2PA initiative

claim: Google Pixel 10 (Sept 2025) signs every photo by default with hardware-backed Titan M2 keys; Sony PXW-Z300 is the first camcorder with native C2PA signing; Nikon Z6 III's C2PA firmware was suspended after a critical signing-key vulnerability and certificates were revoked.
quote: Nikon Z6 III added C2PA via firmware in August 2025, then was suspended after a critical signing vulnerability with all certificates revoked

claim: OpenAI joined the C2PA Steering Committee and attaches Content Credentials to images created and edited by DALL-E 3 in ChatGPT and the OpenAI API.
quote: OpenAI Joins C2PA Steering Committee

claim: EU AI Act Article 50 requires deployers of AI systems generating deepfakes to disclose that the content has been artificially generated or manipulated, clearly and at the time of first exposure.
quote: Deployers of an AI system that generates or manipulates image, audio or video content constituting a deep fake, shall disclose that the content has been artificially generated or manipulated

claim: Article 50 transparency obligations come into force on 2 August 2026 with exemptions for law enforcement and clearly artistic, satirical or fictional works; breaches risk fines up to EUR 15 million or 3% of global turnover.
quote: The transparency obligations in Article 50 of the Act come into force on 2 August 2026

claim: On 17 December 2025 the European Commission published the first draft Code of Practice on Transparency of AI-Generated Content to give providers and deployers practical guidance on Article 50 compliance.
quote: Code of Practice on marking and labelling of AI-generated content

claim: The TAKE IT DOWN Act, signed by President Trump on 19 May 2025, criminalises non-consensual intimate imagery including AI-generated deepfakes and forces platforms to remove flagged content within 48 hours; covered platforms have until 19 May 2026 to stand up the notice-and-removal process.
quote: passed the full House on a 409–2 vote on April 28, 2025... signed into law by President Donald Trump on May 19, 2025

claim: Tennessee's ELVIS Act (signed 21 March 2024) was the first US law to explicitly protect a person's voice (including simulations) from AI cloning and targets both deepfake creators and the providers of tools used to make them; violation is a Class A misdemeanour.
quote: first enacted legislation in the United States specifically designed to protect musicians from the unauthorized use of their voices

claim: California AB 1836 amends the state's right of publicity law to prohibit unauthorized digital replicas of deceased performers in expressive audiovisual works or sound recordings.
quote: California state lawmakers recently passed AB 1836, which would modify the state's right of publicity statute to prohibit the use of digital replicas of a deceased person

claim: Peer-reviewed evaluation shows leading deepfake detectors degrade sharply on cross-dataset and unseen generators — XCeption fell from 89.2% to 85.7% on FaceForensics++ without fine-tuning; MDD's AUC dropped from 0.998 to 0.674 cross-dataset.
quote: XCeption exhibiting the most resilience (accuracy decreased from 89.2% to 85.7%)... MDD declines from 0.998 to 0.674 AUC

claim: A 2025 arXiv study documents systematic 10-15% cross-dataset performance degradation and notes detectors trained on GAN-era data fail badly on diffusion-model outputs, meaning detection vendors are perpetually behind generation.
quote: Deepfake Detection that Generalizes Across Benchmarks

claim: The FBI's IC3 issued a May 2025 PSA warning of an ongoing AI-voice and SMS-based deepfake campaign impersonating senior US officials and recommending out-of-band verification.
quote: Senior US Officials Impersonated in Malicious Messaging Campaign

claim: Norton in July 2025 added on-device audio and visual deepfake protection to its 360 mobile apps via Genie AI, letting users submit clips or YouTube links for authenticity scoring.
quote: Norton Adds Audio and Visual Deepfake Protection on Mobile

claim: Post-Arup industry guidance hardened around mandatory out-of-band callbacks, predetermined authentication codes, and dual-officer counter-signature for any high-value transfer instruction received over video or voice.
quote: verification measures that operate outside the communication channel—such as out-of-band callbacks, predetermined authentication codes or mandatory secondary approval

claim: A 2025 prompt-injection flaw in Gmail's Gemini 'Summarize this email' feature let attackers smuggle invisible white-on-white instructions that produced fake Google security alerts in the AI summary; Gmail still relies on its red 'Don't reply or click links' banner as the primary user-facing warning.
quote: Google Gemini flaw hijacks email summaries for phishing

claim: The Brennan Center frames the 'liar's dividend' — politicians and bad actors dismissing real footage as deepfake — as a growing democratic harm, citing Hany Farid and arguing provenance infrastructure and rapid authentication are the practical counter.
quote: Deepfakes, Elections, and Shrinking the Liar's Dividend

claim: TikTok in 2026 auto-labels AI-generated visuals/audio using C2PA Content Credentials plus invisible watermarks and detection models, has labelled over 1.3 billion AI videos, and demotes or removes unlabelled synthetic media; AI-written captions and hashtags are exempt.
quote: labeled over 1.3 billion AI-generated videos using a combination of Content Credentials, invisible watermarking, and detection models

claim: Meta labels AI-generated content with an 'AI info' tag (replacing the prior 'Made with AI' label in July 2024), driven by self-declaration and metadata signals from partner generators.
quote: Labeling AI Content
